Empires & Puzzles Costumed Karnov 5-Star Legendary Dark Purple from League of Villains Family Review
He's wearing pajamas, holding a toothbrush, and hitting harder than ever. Costumed Karnov is the glow-up this Villain needed. We analyze if his new stats make him viable in the current meta.
Costumed Karnov Review: The Bedtime Bruiser Wakes Up Choosing Violence
Costumed Karnov: The Bedtime Bruiser Wakes Up Choosing Violence
It takes a confident villain to show up to the battlefield in a bathrobe, sleep mask, and holding a toothbrush.

The art team had fun with this one. Gone is the serious, bare-chested brawler. In his place is a man who was clearly woken up from a nap and is

about it. The details—the gold chain clock, the toothbrush held like a shank, the plaid boxers—are fantastic. It’s a hilarious contrast to his devastating special skill. He embodies the energy of "I will destroy you, but first, let me brush my teeth."
This is the glow-up we were waiting for.

Costumed Karnov brings a 2021 hero up to 2025/2026 standards. He is not "broken" or "OP" like the latest 5-star heroes with 3 passives and Fast speed, but he is
With Very Fast speed, his 340% hit + Curse + Board control is devastating.
The stats boost means higher tile damage, and he survives 14* Titans easily.
Usable if you lack other purples, but generally too slow.
The Costume is better in every single metric.
Monk is great for resisting mana ailments.
Fighter is great for reviving and firing one last devastating shot.

Generally, Fighter is preferred for defense/Rush, while Monk might have niche use on offense if you fear Proteus/Hel. But the Costume's stats are so much better you should use the Costume even if you lose the Monk class.
Maximizes his massive health pool and Aether damage reduction. Makes him a brick wall in Rush wars.
Lowers his tile damage for Titans slightly.
1287 Attack is high; emblems push this even higher for massive tile damage.
His primary role is Titans. Even in Rush wars, his high base HP allows him to survive, but you want his special and tiles to hit as hard as possible.
He is finally durable and hits hard.
The best Dark tile buffer in the game, now with stats to survive.
Adds consistent pressure even if the initial hit doesn't kill.
The costume didn't fix his speed. He is a sitting duck in standard mana speed formats.
Outside of Rush and Titans, he struggles to find a spot on rosters deep with Fast heroes.
He shines here. 340% to all at Very Fast speed is scary.
Absolutely mandatory for high-level play against Yellow Titans.
His high HP and AoE damage make him good for clearing waves in events like Tower of Magic (where you get mana buffs).
If they kill Karnov, Kalø fires his skill. If they don't, Karnov buffs the board for Alfrike's cubes.
As always, slow heroes hate Hel, Proteus, and Ruby.
He hits all, so Taunt doesn't stop him completely, but it prevents him from softening the whole team effectively if buffs are blocked.
If you pull this Costume, you have a gem. He will carry you through map stages (high health/AoE damage) and get you into higher Titan loot tiers. Max him ASAP.

You likely have him for Titans already. The Costume makes him safer to use on 14* Titans (no more one-shots) and gives you a viable option for Rush wars. Limit Break 1 is recommended for stats; Limit Break 2 is a luxury unless you really love Rush wars.

Costumed Karnov proves that you can teach an old dog new tricks—or at least put him in pajamas and give him steroids. He has transitioned from a niche, outdated hero into a powerhouse for specific modes. He isn't the most versatile hero in the game, but in his domain (Titans & Rush), he rules supreme.
Karnov is back, and he's... sleepy? Reviewing the 'Bedtime Bruiser' Costume which updates his stats for 2026 and transforms him into a Rush Attack monster.
